hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding with a GRU-based sequence encoder and a cross-attention mechanism is used to model polymer-surface interactions, then it will more accurately predict binding free energies than single-encoder baseline models (DNN or alternative sequence encoders alone), because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the asymmetric, position-dependent complementarity between polymer sequence features and heterogeneous surface patch distributions that governs adsorption affinity.
